title: Survival begins before the emergency
description: Preparation — knowing your duties, your equipment, and your ship — happens before an emergency, not during one.
existingCoverage: Substantially overlaps q-ep-6, q-ep-7 (preparedness vs. response). No new question written.

title: Training reduces fear and improves decision-making
description: Drilled responses reduce hesitation and panic-driven errors when a real emergency occurs.
existingCoverage: Substantially overlaps q-ep-3, q-ep-9, q-ep-12. No new question written.

title: Familiarization with the vessel is essential
description: Every crew member should know the layout of the vessel they're on — escape routes, muster station location, and equipment locations — not just generic emergency procedure.
existingCoverage: New ground — no prior question in the bank.

title: Every crew member has assigned emergency duties
description: A station bill (muster list) assigns specific individuals to specific emergency duties and stations.
existingCoverage: Substantially overlaps q-ep-2, q-ep-8, q-ep-10. No new question written; see the 'station bill' vs. 'muster list' terminology note in import-pack-001.json.

title: SOLAS manuals are vessel-specific
description: A SOLAS training/safety manual is written for a specific vessel's actual equipment and layout — it is not a generic document interchangeable between ships.

title: IMO safety symbols communicate emergency information
description: Standardized IMO pictograms mark safety equipment and muster/escape routes so the information is understood regardless of a crew member's or passenger's spoken language.

title: Common shipboard emergencies
description: Distinct emergency types — fire, foundering (taking on water/sinking), grounding, and hull failure among them — call for recognizably different responses.
existingCoverage: New ground — existing bank has general-alarm questions but no taxonomy of emergency types.

title: Panic reduces survival effectiveness
description: Panic degrades decision-making and slows or derails correct emergency response.
existingCoverage: Substantially overlaps q-ep-3, q-ep-12. No new question written.

title: Drills increase survival readiness
description: Regular practice, not one-time exposure, is what builds real readiness for an emergency.
existingCoverage: Substantially overlaps q-ep-3, q-ep-9. No new question written.

title: Abandon ship drills have required components
description: An abandon ship drill isn't just sounding an alarm — it includes reporting to muster stations, donning lifejackets/immersion suits, and lifeboat lowering/boarding procedure.

title: Fire drills are mandatory
description: Fire drills are a required, regulated part of shipboard safety practice, not an optional exercise.
existingCoverage: New ground — existing bank has a fire-drill-dismissal scenario (q-ep-5) but not the regulatory-mandate framing.

title: Students should ask questions during drills
description: Drills are the appropriate time to clarify anything unclear about your duties or the procedure — not something to save for a real emergency.
